title: Your Brain will kill you Pulling the Spears and Realism
display_title: Your Brain will kill you Pulling the Spears and Realism
wikitext:
[]: Your Brain will kill you: Pulling the Spears and Realism Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 14, 2013 7:53 am Consider the phenomena discribed in this article: http://www.cracked.com/blog/5-beliefs-about-surviving-disaster-that-can-kill-you_p2/ Especially the one that links this article: http://blogs.mprnews.org/newscut/2012/07/in_a_crisis_your_brain_may_be/ Evaluate mortal danger/stress factor induced by Shinji vs. Asuka battle. Discuss relevance to spears incident. (or lack thereof)

[]: Post by ElMariachi » Sat Dec 14, 2013 10:45 am So, in short : Shinji's brain was so stressed and overloaded by the fight in Lilith's Chamber that he ignored every warnings around him and only believed his eyes. Well yes, of course, that was the whole point of Gendo crushing him by guilt and reducing him to an insomniac mental wreck : so when the inevitable confrontation against WILLE comes, the added stress of fighting his former friends makes him enters in panic mode and ignore every warnings telling him to stop, even if it comes from his best(and now only) friend in the world. Shinji always had a lot of difficulties to trust anyone, you could even say that he never really trusted anyone in his whole life, a trust deep enough so he would take said person's advice above his own, chalk it as a consequence of both his solitary childhood raised by a stranger and his natural stubbornness. And that's something he'll have to learn to correct : to start taking other's people advice into account, especially when he's in a situation completely over his head. To paraphrase the concluding phrase of your second link "Sometimes the best thing you can do, is step back and take a breath.", Shinji failed to do that, and he and Kaworu paid the price of it.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 14, 2013 3:55 pm ElMariachi wrote: To paraphrase the concluding phrase of your second link "Sometimes the best thing you can do, is step back and take a breath.", Shinji failed to do that, and he and Kaworu paid the price of it.

[]: Post by Rei IV » Sat Dec 14, 2013 4:40 pm Is this suppose to persuade (us) viewers that Shinji's actions in the pulling the spears, despite ignoring Kaworu's warning and the consequences that would follow, is justified?

[]: Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Dec 14, 2013 4:59 pm Maybe not justified but certainly understandable. Desperate people will do things that a person in a better mental state surely wouldn't. Even if this is a biology tangent it still feels like this thread will turn into yet another blame game.

[]: Post by pwhodges » Sat Dec 14, 2013 5:15 pm Rei IV wrote: Is this suppose to persuade (us) viewers that Shinji's actions in the pulling the spears, despite ignoring Kaworu's warning and the consequences that would follow, is justified?

[]: Post by Fireball » Sat Dec 14, 2013 5:27 pm You know a film is badly written when its fans have to use cracked articles to defend it.

[]: Post by Giji Shinka » Sat Dec 14, 2013 5:28 pm Fireball wrote: You know a film is badly written when its fans have to use cracked articles to defend it.

[]: Post by pwhodges » Sat Dec 14, 2013 5:48 pm Fireball wrote: You know a film is badly written when its fans have to use cracked articles to defend it.

[]: Post by ElMariachi » Sat Dec 14, 2013 6:31 pm Kendrix wrote: I feel this was kinda what Fuyu was alluding to with that quote about shogi, but the point I'm trying to make, it's easier said than done; Shinji isn't really made the way Fuyutsuki or Gendo are, and he didn't really have the time/intense training to override that. Especially not when an opponent he really doesn't want to seriously injure just came at him with a polearm - and is going to come after him once she's finished recharging.

[]: Post by WunderBah » Sat Dec 14, 2013 7:41 pm Fireball wrote: You know a film is badly written when its fans have to use cracked articles to defend it.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 14, 2013 7:57 pm pwhodges wrote: No - it's meant to point out that probably you or I would have done no better, whatever we might think, so criticising him from our armchairs could be seen as bordering on hypocritical.

[]: Post by Fireball » Sat Dec 14, 2013 8:23 pm pwhodges wrote: Well, obviously it's written for effect (as is your comment); but some of the linked articles are a little less dismissible - I don't automatically discount the contents of articles in pubmed, for instance.

[]: Post by Rei IV » Sat Dec 14, 2013 8:36 pm Fireball wrote: Maybe Shinji's mindset would be more understandable, like in series, if the scene beforehand wouldn't have been so incredibly forced. Desperation is one thing, being written so you have to be a stupid for the sake of it is something else.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 14, 2013 9:03 pm Compared to the examples giving in the article (where there was even actual explanation) the Q thing doean't really look that contrived. Again, Asuka's & Mari's attack was a huge factor that shouldn't be underestimated. This is where, at least for me, the whole horror factor of the movie comes from. It's not movie stupidity, it's sad reality (same goes, of course, for WILLE. The news are full of cases of people turning on others for less rational reasons. Misato, Asuka etc needn't be evil, either - just finite. Certainly very wrong when seen from an outside perspective, but not inherently evil/selfish/stupid/judgmental etc.) In that sense it was really useful to see Q with a fresh dub to jostle the pre-saved perceptions of it (in which i was,like most of the ppl here, pre-spoiled) When you look at the whole Kaworu deal, it's really just cryptic pharases and him sort of retreating into deep thought - Alarm only becomes really apparent when Shinji's climbing lillith's back and the audience sees the doom comming already... He was always in his own, detached sphere; Shinji was constantly not getting what he was saying back in ep 24, allthough he only minded when the angel bit was revealed and things became relevant. To turn that against him is just a logical conclusion of that, ain't it? There's not really any other way to drive a wedge between the two without distorting their characters. Did Kaworu really know what will happen, or just that thing's aren't as SEELE told him, and that this can't mean anything good? He#s used to always knowing stuff. He promised Shinji (who was pretty much falling appart on him without Kaworu being able to do anything - and you can tell in the pre-departure scene that it affects him) he could fix things for him. Moreover, he can probably guess that they've been lured into a trap specifically tailored to exploit their weaknesses. And Shinji's only here because Kaworu convinced him to. You probably have substantial parts of the entire thing going on in reverse here, and they ARE supposed to be parallels after all.

[]: Post by WunderBah » Sat Dec 14, 2013 9:24 pm Rei IV wrote: This is something where I can say I understand were you're coming from. Basically, Shinji pulls a stupid move because "the plot demands it" or the "idiot ball" comes into place, leaving us with a "WTF?" reaction. It does give viewers the impression there is some hamfisted point is to be made or that it feels all too contrived. Hell, I love Q but I won't deny I had similar impressions you've have as well.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 14, 2013 9:45 pm In any case, wouldn't inserting some huge internal monologue or leadup to what is supposed to be at best a gut decision to take a particular risk and at worst a badly though-out part sparked by your inner monkey, lack of sleep and copious quantities of desperation - if there was time for monologueing, we wouldn't have this mess in the first place. Remember when Naruto Shippuuden stretched out three in-universe minutes to two episodes? This is not one of these animes. Note that he didn't even think of smashing the battery pack Mari just threw Asuka, although that would certainly at least hinder her pursuit. That's how out of it he was.

[]: Post by ElMariachi » Sun Dec 15, 2013 8:09 am Rei IV wrote: This is something where I can say I understand were you're coming from. Basically, Shinji pulls a stupid move because "the plot demands it" or the "idiot ball" comes into place, leaving us with a "WTF?" reaction. It does give viewers the impression there is some hamfisted point is to be made or that it feels all too contrived. Hell, I love Q but I won't deny I had similar impressions you've have as well.

[]: Post by WunderBah » Sun Dec 15, 2013 10:23 am ElMariachi wrote: I admit I feel like this a little. I understand where Anno wanted to go : Shinji was so destroyed previously that the fight in Lilith's Chamber was the last straw that broke the camel's back and made him enter into instinct/panic mode in which he will obsessively go for the spears in the hope that it'll fix everything, only trusting his eyes instead of the aural warning, to retake what the articles in the OP said. But he had been too subtle with that, he should have insisted a little more on how much Shinji was on the brink of a complete mental collapse, because after the scene where Kaworu convinces him to pilot, he seems back to his normal self, maybe having added lines under his eyes(since they seemed to have magically disappeared when inside EVA-13), see Shinji occasionally dozing off during their descent through the main shaft to show him fighting against the accumulated fatigue of various sleepless nights, or having him repeat to himself "I'll fix everything and everyone will forgive me", "Don't worry everything will be fine, I can do it" like a crazy mantra to showcase how ridden by guilt and desperate for atonement he is.

[]: Post by Giji Shinka » Sun Dec 15, 2013 12:13 pm ElMariachi wrote: It's true that the situation ended with the spears pulled out thanks to an unlikely combination of Shinji on the edge of insanity, Asuka completely unwilling to even try to reason with Shinji, and Kaworu for some reason going into autism while Asuka is trying to skew them with a giant naginata! That feels really forced.
